title: Daily Briefing — May 07
headline: Mega-cap tech rallies hard, but the party is thinning — breadth and sector risk up, flows drive everything.
body: Right, so today's data shows tech leading again: AAPL $287.51 and NVDA $207.83 both torching their 20 MA, with QQQ and SPY breaking out ($695.77 and $733.83 respectively). VIX is cooling off, but don't let the calm fool you — last month's Fear & Greed was panic (27.6), now it's 68.6 (greed), creeping toward extreme territory.

On rotation, XLK (Tech) is still the low-risk, leading sector — RS 113.2, Mom 105.0 — but almost everything else is lagging or outright sick. XLE (Energy) and XLRE (Real Estate) are weakening. Financials (XLF $51.84) are stuck below the 200 MA ($52.30), classic canary signal, so risk remains elevated in non-tech.

Contrarian radar: When sentiment swings this fast from 'fear' to 'greed', be suspicious. Back in 2021, similar Smart Money/Dumb Money extremes set up the 2022 drawdown. Energy names showing active breakdown (SM -8.5%, AR -5.8%) as tech runs hot — that's rotational divergence. Abundance mindset here: don't chase, watch for mean reversion at weekly 20 MA if tech gets vertical.
callout_body:
AAPL: 20 MA $270.22 — holding, strong
NVDA: $207.83 (20 MA $200.47) — breakout
SPY: $733.83 (20 MA $707.57) — momentum pivot
XLF: $51.84 (200 MA $52.30) — canary on watch
XLK: RS 113.2, momentum 105.0 — leader sector
Fear & Greed: 68.6 — approaching contrarian zone
XLE: $57.00 (200 MA $48.24) — sellers active
VIX: 17.43 — low, but monitor mean reversion
action_items: Tech breakout trades: NVDA, AAPL, QQQ above 20 MA — monitor for flow exhaustion or dark pool accumulation, don't chase highs.
Rotate out of lagging risk: Watch Financials (XLF), Energy (XLE), and Healthcare (XLV) for further breakdown or sector recovery at weekly 20 MA.
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price—wait for the flows to paint the next move.
